data.title: AST SpaceMobile continues to rally as investors eye the role Amazon and Jeff Bezos could play
data.description:
AST SpaceMobile (NASDAQ:ASTS) extended its recent rally on Wednesday as investors continue to eye the company's relationship with Amazon (AMZN). The buzz went into overdrive after AST SpaceMobile (NASDAQ:ASTS) board member Adriana Cisneros posted a picture on Instagram with Jeff Bezos titled "Amazing things are happening at AST & Science + @blueorigin". Notably, Cisneros was AST SpaceMobile's (ASTS) first-ever institutional investor and is currently a board member. 

Scotiabank analyst Andres Coello highlighted that there are major areas of opportunity where AST SpaceMobile (ASTS), Amazon (AMZN), and Blue Origin (BORGN) could collaborate, particularly regarding Project Kuiper, which intends to address the fixed broadband opportunity. 

"Could Jeff Bezos become an ASTS investor? This is a possibility widely discussed by investors and analysts, in no small measure because Bezos is one of the world's top space investors," wrote Coello.

It was noted that while Amazon (AMZN) intends to invest $13 billion to $16 billion in the Kuiper constellation of 3,236 satellites, the current market capitalization of ASTS stands at only $8.3 billion. Notably, the firm's revenue projection for ASTS is actually larger than for Kuiper at $30 billion by 2033 vs. the $25 billion estimated for Kuiper. Looking ahead, Scotiabank said it would not be surprised if both groups are exploring a larger agreement that could involve New Glenn's support for BB2 launches and possible commercial/equity agreements between ASTS and Amazon (AMZN).

Shares of AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) are up 5.3% in morning trading. Over the last week, the satellite manufacturer stock is up more than 15%.

data.title: Trump's 'big, beautiful bill' comes with a $2.42T price tag, CBO estimates
data.description:
The nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office estimated Wednesday that U.S. President Donald Trump's tax-and-spending package -- narrowly passed by House Republicans last month and now facing resistance in the Senate -- would add $2.42T to fiscal budget deficits over the next 10 years.

The bill would reduce the federal government's revenues by $3.67T and decrease outlays by $1.25T over the decade through 2034, according to the CBO's calculations.

The projections come as concerns about the sustainability of the U.S.'s fiscal path heat up.

On Tuesday, Just days after Elon Musk left Washington, DC, and his post heading the Department of Government Efficiency, the richest man in the world harshly criticized the “One Big Beautiful Bill" that Trump is urging Republican senators to back, calling it a "disgusting abomination."

Trump is set to gather with Senate Finance Republicans on Wednesday to talk through the bill.

Note that the $2.42T figure from the CBO excludes any dynamic scoring, meaning the deficit-increase projection does not factor in potential economic growth or other secondary effects from tax and spending changes.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has pushed back against growing worries about rising U.S. debt on the view that an acceleration in economic growth would help offset the expected revenue headwind from the bill.

data.title: Natera's Signatera test to be covered under Medicare
data.description:
• Natera (NASDAQ:NTRA) said on Wednesday that its genome-based Signatera molecular residual disease test is now covered by Medicare. 
  • The company said that this coverage, under LCD L38779, applies to beneficiaries with colorectal cancer, breast cancer, bladder cancer, ovarian cancer, lung cancer, and pan-cancer immunotherapy monitoring.
  • The coverage is similar to previously covered Signatera indications.

data.title: Triumph Gold to acquire Coyote Knoll Silver-Gold Mineral property
data.description:
• Triumph Gold (OTCPK:TIGCF) on Wednesday announced the acquisition of the Coyote Knoll Silver ((Ag)) - Gold ((Au)) property, located in central Utah.
  • Triumph Gold has entered into an agreement to purchase the Coyote Knoll Silver-Gold property for the sum of $150,000 and the issuance of one million common shares of the company.

data.title: Bayer subsidiary gains worldwide rights to develop cancer asset VVD-214
data.description:
• Bayer (OTCPK:BAYRY) subsidiary Vividion Therapeutics has gained the worldwide rights to develop and commercialize the Werner helicase covalent inhibitor VVD-214.
  • The asset has been developed between Vividion and Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) under a collaboration and license agreement inked in 2020.
  • A phase 1 trial currently under way is examining VVD-214 as monotherapy and in combination with Keytruda (pembrolizumab) for patients with solid tumors that display high MSI or deficient mismatch repair including colorectal, endometrial, ovarian, and gastric cancers.
  • Bayer (OTCPK:BAYZF) noted that Werner helicase is" a DNA repair enzyme and a highly sought-after synthetic lethal target for treatment of cancers with microsatellite instability. By inhibiting WRN, VVD-214 aims to cause lethal DNA damage in cancers with high microsatellite instability while minimizing harm to healthy cells."

data.title: Trump again urges Powell to cut rates after soft private sector job growth
data.description:
President Donald Trump once again urged Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell to lower interest rates after private hiring in May reached its lowest level in over two years. 

U.S. private sector employment grew by 37K in May, missing the +110K consensus and cooling from the 62K increase in April, according to data released by ADP on Wednesday. It was the lowest monthly job total from the ADP count since March 2023. 

Trump's latest criticism of Powell comes about a week after he met the central bank chief "to discuss economic developments, including growth, employment, and inflation".

"ADP NUMBER OUT!!! 'Too Late' Powell must now LOWER THE RATE. He is unbelievable!!! Europe has lowered NINE TIMES!" Trump posted on Truth Social. 

Trump has been ramping up his criticism of Powell for being too slow to cut interest rates, and there were reports of him considering Powell's termination. He later said he had "no intention" of removing Powell.

Last month, Trump had called Powell a "fool" a day after the central bank held its key interest rate steady, as widely expected.

The Fed reduced its federal funds rate target range by 100 basis points starting in December but has held the rates at its current range of 4.25%-4.50% since the beginning of 2025.

Powell's term as chair expires in May 2026.

Stock index futures earlier ticked lower after the ADP data but then recovered. Wall Street subsequently opened with slight gains.  

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Post Holdings earns high marks from analysts for its 8th Avenue Food & Provisions takeover
data.description:
Post Holdings (NYSE:POST) is being sized up by analysts after its deal to acquire 8th Avenue Food & Provisions for ~$880 million. The St. Louis-based company said the acquisition internalizes the manufacturing of Post's (NYSE:POST) Peter Pan peanut butter, represents its entry into the dry pasta category with leading brand Ronzoni, and enables greater participation in the growing granola sub-category of ready-to-eat cereal. 

8th Avenue was established in October 2018 as a strategic blend of Post Holdings’ private brand businesses, in partnership with the private equity firm Thomas H. Lee Partners. The company has grown through several key acquisitions, including a peanut butter processing plant in Illinois and the Ronzoni pasta brand. Of note, Post Holdings (NYSE:POST) already held a 60.5% ownership stake before announcing the complete takeover.

The acquisition is expected to be completed by July 1

Evercore ISI boosted its price target on Post Holdings (POST) to $131 following the deal. "Over time, we can imagine the nut butter business as a foundation for further organic growth (e.g. high protein) and a platform for synergistic acquisitions," wrote analyst David Palmer.

Mizuho Securities analyst John Baumgartner said positives from the deal include that Post Holdings (POST) knows 8th Avenue well, which limits execution risk. The steady financial returns and free cash flow accretion potential were also seen as positive. Notably, the pro forma net leverage 4.6X should not preclude additional M&A from the food company, per Baumgartner. Mizuho kept an Outperform rating on Post (POST). 

Wells Fargo analyst Marc Torrente highlighted that Post (POST) is a known entity providing scale in complementary categories/price-points that enhance the portfolio. "Strategically and financially seems like a good use of capital, with flexibility to continue opportunistic allocation," he noted.

Shares of Post Holdings (POST) were up 1.0% in early trading on Wednesday. On a year-to-date basis, the food stock is down 3.7%. Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street analysts have a consensus Buy rating on POST. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating is also flashing Buy.

data.title: Neogen falls after Q4 update
data.description:
Neogen Corporation (NASDAQ:NEOG) lost ~14% in the morning hours on Wednesday after the food safety player updated its Q4 fiscal 2025 outlook in conjunction with a presentation at the ongoing William Blair Growth Stock Conference in Chicago, Illinois.

While the Lansing, Michigan-based healthcare supplier cited preliminary data to announce that its Q4 revenue will approach guidance, the company projected temporary headwinds affecting its gross margin.

However, NEOG said its adjusted EBITDA is expected to hit the high teens range even as market conditions remain weak, with Q3 softness extending to Q4.

The company also projected nearly $5M in impact related to tariffs and announced plans to market its genomics business as the second divestiture.

In April, Neogen (NASDAQ:NEOG) agreed to sell its global cleaners and disinfectants business to Kersia Group. The deal worth $130M remains on track to close in Q4 FY26, the company said.
